title: Add `extensions.forceInstall` setting to auto-install workspace extensions
body:
Add `extensions.forceInstall` and `extensions.disableForceInstall` configuration settings for automatically installing extensions as workspace-scoped extensions.

Each extension version is force-installed at most once per workspace. When the target version changes (new gallery version or updated workspace resource extension), force installation runs again.

Closes #299826
Closes #299039

title: Add `extensions.forceInstall` setting to auto-install workspace extensions

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: extensions
labels.description: Issues concerning extensions

labels.name: verification-needed
labels.description: Verification of issue is requested

body:
Add a new `extensions.forceInstall` configuration setting that automatically installs a list of extensions as workspace-scoped extensions.

**Key behaviors:**
- Extensions listed in `extensions.forceInstall` are automatically installed as workspace extensions.
- Each extension version is force-installed at most once per workspace — if the version hasn't changed, it won't be reinstalled on subsequent startups.
- When a new version becomes available (gallery or workspace resource), force installation runs again.
- Users can opt out of individual extensions via the companion `extensions.disableForceInstall` setting.
- Supports both gallery extensions and workspace resource extensions (from `.vscode/extensions/`).

## Verification steps

**Gallery extension:**
1. Add `"extensions.forceInstall": ["ms-vscode.js-debug-nightly"]` to workspace settings.
2. Reload — extension installs as workspace extension.
3. Uninstall the extension, reload — should not be installed again.

**Workspace resource extension:**
1. Check `.vscode/extensions/` for a local extension (e.g. the selfhost test extension).
2. Add its ID to `extensions.forceInstall`.
3. Reload — installs from workspace resource.
4. Uninstall the extension, reload — should not be installed again.
5. Bump the version in the workspace resource extension's `package.json`, reload — should be installed again.

**Opt-out:**
1. Add the extension ID to `extensions.disableForceInstall` — it should no longer be force-installed.

Closes #299039

title: Space character typed in Textual does not appear in VS Code integrated terminal

labels.name: new release
labels.description: Issues found in a recent release of VS Code

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.110
- OS Version: macOS 26.3 

Steps to Reproduce:

_Originally reported in the Textual repo: https://github.com/Textualize/textual/issues/6408_

- Using Textual 8.0.2, Python 3.12
- Given the following Python code:
```py
from textual.app import App
from textual.widgets import Input


class TestApp(App):
    def compose(self):
        yield Input(placeholder="Type here...")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    app = TestApp()
    app.run()
```

When I press the spacebar the space character does not get displayed when running inside the VS Code integrated terminal:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ad4c8c24-5b4d-4104-8ebd-b7530625281f


It works if I disable the `terminal.integrated.enableKittyKeyboardProtocol` setting in VS Code. This started happening in VS Code version 1.110.

From https://github.com/Textualize/textual/issues/6408#issuecomment-4013027855:

> Based on this sentence from the [Disambiguate escape codes](https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/keyboard-protocol/#disambiguate-escape-codes) section:
> 
> > With this flag turned on, all key events **that do not generate text** are represented in one of the following two forms [emphasis mine]
> 
> My understanding is that space should not be reported with `CSI u` encoding.

title: groups component explorer updates
Claude Code Third-Party Agents conflicts with ~.claude/settings.json env vars

labels.name: new release
labels.description: Issues found in a recent release of VS Code

body:
Claude Code Third-Party Agents conflicts with ~.claude/settings.json env vars
 
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.38.1
- VS Code Version: 1.110.0
- OS Version: Windows 11
- Feature: Third-Party Agents (Claude Code)
- Selected model: Claude 4.x 

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Have global ~.claude/settings.json with `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` and/or `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` environment variables populated
  - potentially relevant vscode-chat repo search [results](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Amicrosoft%2Fvscode-copilot-chat+ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL&type=code)
2. Execute command with Claude Code 3rd Party agent (docs: https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/agents/third-party-agents#_claude-agent-preview )
3. Command processes indefinitely, never hits GH Copilot endpoint and finishes
  - can verify removing the `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` and/or `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` from ~.claude/settings.json and reloading chat windows with Claude Code third-party agent, then completes successfully as expected

suggestions for fix, either:
- embed coded workaround to just ignore these particular env var settings when is executed through this copilot chat interface to claude code agent
- or, allow setting of `claudeCode.environmentVariables` available setting in user settings json to override `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` and/or `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` with `null/""` value to signify ignoring value in global ~.claude/settings.json  _(tried this workaround, but did not work)_

title: test: persist chat sessions sidebar section collapse state
body:
Refs: #299355

- [ ] anyOS
- [ ] anyOS

Complexity: 2

Authors: @daviddossett

---

**Requirements:**
- Have enough chat sessions to populate multiple time-based sections (Today, Yesterday, Last 7 Days, etc.)

**Testing:**

1. **Section collapse state persists across view toggles**
   - [ ] Open the chat sessions sidebar
   - [ ] Collapse a section that is expanded by default (e.g. "Today")
   - [ ] Expand a section that is collapsed by default (e.g. "More")
   - [ ] Close and reopen the sidebar
   - [ ] Verify the sections retain their collapse/expand state

2. **Section collapse state persists across restarts**
   - [ ] Set some sections to non-default collapse states
   - [ ] Restart VS Code
   - [ ] Verify the sections retain their collapse/expand state from before the restart

3. **Multiple sections persist independently**
   - [ ] Collapse "Today" and expand "Yesterday" (or other available sections)
   - [ ] Close and reopen the sidebar
   - [ ] Verify each section independently remembers its state

4. **Programmatic collapse changes do not overwrite user state**
   - [ ] Manually expand a section
   - [ ] Trigger a refresh of the sessions list (e.g. by creating a new session or toggling the unread filter)
   - [ ] Verify the section remains in the state you set, not reset to the default

5. **Default behavior still works when no saved state exists**
   - [ ] Clear storage or use a fresh profile
   - [ ] Open the chat sessions sidebar
   - [ ] Verify default collapse behavior applies (e.g. "More" section collapsed, recent sections expanded)

title: Critical Error 400: Unsupported value 'xhigh' for model 'gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07' (ARM64/WSL)

labels.name: new release
labels.description: Issues found in a recent release of VS Code

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version:  1.110.0
- OS Version: Windows 11 Home (Build 26200.7840) -> Though, I use a WSL on my Windows on ARM 

I keep getting this error when trying to run the agent using the Github Copilot Chat extension:
```Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.
Copilot Request id: d034c544-88d9-400c-b7f0-99da542b2c6b
GH Request Id: 5F11:48962:6C08BB5:78BA6D3:69AB0A9F
Reason: Request Failed: 400 {"error":{"message":"Unsupported value: 'xhigh' is not supported with the 'gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07' model. Supported values are: 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', and 'high'.","code":"invalid_request_body"}}
```

**Steps to Reproduce:**
1. Open VS Code on a Windows on ARM host and connect to a WSL2 instance (e.g., Ubuntu).
2. Ensure the GitHub Copilot Chat extension is active (reproduces on both Stable and Pre-Release versions).
3. Open the Copilot Chat pane and enter any prompt (e.g., "Hello" or a coding question).
4. Observe the immediate failure: The chat returns a 400 Bad Request error instead of a response.
5. Check the output logs/developer tools to confirm the client is incorrectly requesting the model gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07 with the unsupported parameter xhigh.

title: Ensures ColorId.DefaultBackground is set when no colors are registered.
dispose of all terminals for session on dispose or archive
body:
fixes #298890

1. Fixed terminal tracking from “one terminal per session” to “all terminals per session.”
2. On chat session cleanup, we now dispose every tracked terminal for that session, not just the last one.
3. Added cleanup when a session is archived, since archive does not trigger the dispose event path.
4. Kept backward-compatible fallback cleanup for cases where only the old single-terminal map is populated.
5. Added tests for both:
- multiple terminals disposed on session dispose
- multiple terminals disposed on session archive

cc @tyriar

Question: are sessions growing unbounded and isn't that an issue? I don't see a hard cap.

title: Fixes problem with code coverage on windows
Regression: PDF Git diff original revision fails to load with PDF.js 401 in 1.110 (works in 1.109.5)

labels.name: new release
labels.description: Issues found in a recent release of VS Code

body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.110.0 - OS Version: Darwin arm64 25.3.0 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a new Git repository. 2. Add and commit a simple PDF file, for example `simple.pdf`. 3. Modify `simple.pdf` and save it again with the same filename. 4. Open the repository in VS Code. 5. In the Source Control view, open the Git diff for `simple.pdf`. ```python import matplotlib.pyplot as plt fig, ax = plt.subplots() ax.plot([0, 1], [0, 1]) fig.savefig("simple.pdf") ``` Expected: - Both the original and modified PDF revisions should load. Actual: - The modified/current PDF on the right loads correctly. - The original/previous revision on the left fails with a PDF.js 401 error from a `git+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/...` URL. **1.110.0** fail **1.109.5** work

title: GitHub Copilot conversation compaction effectiveness severely drops in v1.110
body:
In v1.110 **conversation summarization/compaction does not reclaim much context window space**. For example, immediately after summarizing the conversation of Claude Open 4.6 (the first compaction in the conversation), **the context window is still 50% full**! This is hardly useful. Previous versions of GitHub Copilot would result in perhaps 20% or less (if I remember correctly) of the context window being full directly after context summarization.

I manually invoke `/compact` immediately after compaction, the context window drops down only slightly, to e.g. 36% full.

Is this the expected workflow in v1.110—that compaction not do much, but that a user needs to manually invoke `/compact` repeatedly to slowly reclaim context window space, which would have automatically been reclaimed in previous versions?

```
Version: 1.110.0 (user setup)
Commit: 0870c2a0c7c0564e7631bfed2675573a94ba4455
Date: 2026-03-04T01:20:28.863Z
Electron: 39.6.0
ElectronBuildId: 13330601
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.0
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
```
